meta.label: Node.js
meta.subject: nodejs
meta.description: Restructured Node.js and Express.js learning dataset. Combined source headings were split into individual topics. Practical prompts mixed into interview data were merged, completed with implementation fields, and placed under the correct topics.

examples: Modules, CommonJS, ES Modules, npm, package files, dependencies, and environment variables are separate.
FS, Path, OS, URL, Crypto, Util, Zlib, and Timers are separate.
HTTP fundamentals, methods, request/response, headers, status codes, HTTPS, and networking are separate.
Express fundamentals, app methods, routing, parameters, body parsing, static files, and file uploads are separate.
Authentication, authorization, JWT, sessions, cookies, and browser storage are separate.
Event loop, libuv, async I/O, concurrency, worker threads, child processes, and clusters are separate.
Streams, buffers, piping, events, EventEmitter, and event-driven architecture are separate.
Security topics such as hashing, CSRF, XSS, CSP, Helmet, and SQL injection are separate.

practiceQuestions.title: Start a Node.js Application on Port 3000
practiceQuestions.task: Create a server that reads PORT from the environment, falls back to 3000, and logs the listening URL after startup.
practiceQuestions.starterCode:
const PORT = Number(process.env.PORT) || 3000;

// TODO: create the server and listen on PORT
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Use process.env.PORT and provide a numeric fallback.
sourceQuestions: How to start a nodejs application in port 3000 (practical)

notes.text: Your main server keeps running smoothly

topics.name: Worker Threads

practiceQuestions.title: Offload a CPU-Heavy Task to a Worker Thread

notes.text: But sometimes you need to do CPU-heavy tasks like:
notes.sourceTopic: Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling
Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling

notes.text: Those tasks block the single thread and can slow down your entire server. 🔧 45. That’s where Worker Threads come in.
notes.sourceTopic: Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling
Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling
Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling

notes.text: Say you’re building an Express server and you want to process a huge JSON file. Without worker threads, it would block all other requests.
notes.sourceTopic: Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling
Worker Threads, Cluster, Child Process & Scaling

notes.text: You offload that task to a new thread

Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Listen for data, end, and error events, or use for await...of.

practiceQuestions.title: Transfer Data Between Files with pipe()
practiceQuestions.task: Copy data from a source file to a destination file using readable.pipe(writable) or pipeline().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: pipeline() forwards errors and resolves when copying is complete.
sourceQuestions: transfer data from one file to another using pipe

practiceQuestions.title: Implement a Simple EventEmitter Flow
practiceQuestions.task: Create an EventEmitter, subscribe to a custom event, emit it with data, and demonstrate the difference between on() and once().
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Emit the same event twice after registering both on() and once().

topics.name: View Engines & EJS
interviewQuestions: How to fetch an api from nodejs

practiceQuestions.title: Configure EJS and Render Dynamic Data
practiceQuestions.task: Configure EJS as the view engine and render a homepage that receives a username or email from the route handler.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Use app.set("view engine", "ejs") and res.render(view, data).
sourceQuestions: workout: display current user's email id in the home page

notes.sourceTopic: View Engines, EJS, SSR & Template Rendering

notes.text: Explore NodeJS in a structured and sequential way.
notes.sourceTopic: View Engines, EJS, SSR & Template Rendering

topics.name: Server-Side Rendering
interviewQuestions: server side rendering vs client side rendering

topics.name: Error Handling & Error-Handling Middleware
interviewQuestions: How to do exception handling in nodejs
how to handle unhandled errors in nodejs
error handling using custom event emitter
how error handling middleware is invoked
Explain how error handling works in Node.js.

practiceQuestions.title: Create Express Error-Handling Middleware
practiceQuestions.task: Create four-argument Express error middleware that returns a consistent JSON error response.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: The signature must be (err, req, res, next), and the middleware should be registered last.
sourceQuestions: Types of middleware, custom, and error handling middleware - practical
how to setup an error handling middleware

notes.text: General Terms, Notes & Small Fragments No items.

topics.name: Debugging, Profiling & Testing
interviewQuestions: Debugging JavaScript (browser dev tools Node.js debugging)

topics.name: Security Fundamentals
interviewQuestions: Why do we need validation on both sides

practiceQuestions.title: Encrypt and Decrypt a String
practiceQuestions.task: Encrypt a UTF-8 string with Node.js crypto using an authenticated cipher and decrypt it back to the original value.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Use AES-GCM with a random IV and preserve the authentication tag.
sourceQuestions: Encrypt a string in node js

practiceQuestions.title: Hash and Verify a Password with bcrypt
practiceQuestions.task: Hash a password with bcrypt and verify a login candidate with bcrypt.compare(). Never decrypt or store the plaintext password.
constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Use a reasonable salt-round value and await both operations.

practiceQuestions.title: Write hashed data to a file using fs mofule
practiceQuestions.task: write hashed data to a file using fs mofule

sampleInput.description: Use the scenario described in the task.

expectedOutput.description: The requested behavior is completed without unhandled errors.

constraints: Use modern Node.js APIs.
Handle expected errors and return an appropriate result.
practiceQuestions.hint: Break the task into setup, input handling, core logic, and error handling.
sourceQuestions: write hashed data to a file using fs mofule
